pr.title: Split & link some files from gm_1A4C
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Split the tail of src/melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c into three newly linked game-mode units: gmprogressive.c, gmapproach.c, and gm_1ADD.c. The PR updated GALE01 split ranges, added new public headers, moved private structs/statics out of gm_1A4C.static.h into the owning C files, wired the new headers into scene data users, and marked the new objects as Matching in configure.py. decomp.dev reported +8048 linked bytes and +3104 matched-code bytes, with 22 functions newly matching in the new units; the apparent broken matches in gm_1A4C were expected relocation effects from splitting.

key_files.role: Narrowed gm_1A4C ranges and added contiguous section ranges for gmprogressive.c, gmapproach.c, and gm_1ADD.c.
Added Matching objects for melee/gm/gmprogressive.c, melee/gm/gmapproach.c, and melee/gm/gm_1ADD.c; also switched gmmenu.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Removed approximately 652 lines from the original aggregate file after gm_801ACF8C_OnFrame.
New unit for the progressive scan prompt logic around gm_801AD088, gm_801AD254, gm_801AD620_OnFrame, gm_801AD874_OnEnter, and gm_801AD8EC_OnLeave.
New unit for approach scene setup/on-frame/on-enter/on-leave logic around fn_801AD920, gm_801ADB04, gm_801ADC88_OnFrame, gm_801ADCE4_OnEnter, and gm_801ADDA8_OnLeave.
New unit for the later gm_1ADD range, including list/text/object helper logic such as gm_801ADDD8, gm_801ADE1C, gm_801AE050, fn_801AE948, gm_801AEBB0, gm_801AECC4, and gm_801AEDC8.
Removed declarations that now belong to gmprogressive.h, gmapproach.h, or gm_1ADD.h.
Removed private struct/static declarations that moved into the new owning C file.
New public header exposing only the progressive scene callbacks.
New public header exposing only the approach scene callbacks.
New public header exposing gm_1ADD helper APIs used by neighboring game-mode code.
Updated includes to consume gm_1ADD.h after the split.
Included gmapproach.h and gmprogressive.h so scene data references resolve to the split units.

what_changed: The original gm_1A4C.c text range was reduced from 0x801A6254-0x801AEE6C to 0x801A6254-0x801AD088.
New split ranges were added: gmprogressive.c at text 0x801AD088-0x801AD920, gmapproach.c at 0x801AD920-0x801ADDD8, and gm_1ADD.c at 0x801ADDD8-0x801AEE6C.
Data, bss, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 ranges were split alongside text ranges, keeping section ownership contiguous with the new C files.
gmprogressive.c owns the static state gm_80480D70 and keeps internal setup/state helper functions static while exporting only OnFrame, OnEnter, and OnLeave callbacks.
gmapproach.c owns the static state gm_80480D98 and keeps fn_801AD920 and gm_801ADB04 static while exporting the scene callbacks.
gm_1ADD.c owns the linked-list/text-object state struct gm_803DD4D0 plus gm_804D6868 and gm_804D686C, replacing their previous placement in gm_1A4C.static.h.
gm_1A4C.h stopped declaring functions from the split-out ranges, and three narrower headers were added for the new units.
configure.py links the three new files as Matching objects.
The decomp.dev report recorded 22 new matches in the new units and overall increases of +3104 matched code bytes and +8048 linked code bytes.
smart_moves: The split followed exact address boundaries and mirrored all affected sections, not just .text, reducing the risk of data-order drift.
Private per-scene state was moved into the owning new C file instead of remaining in gm_1A4C.static.h, improving local ownership and avoiding unnecessary cross-file statics.
Only callback/API functions needed by other files were exposed in the new headers; local setup helpers remained static.
Scene-data users were updated to include gmapproach.h and gmprogressive.h rather than continuing to depend on the broad gm_1A4C.h.

The split converted previously partially/unmatched code in the aggregate gm_1A4C unit into fully matching standalone objects, which decomp.dev confirmed across functions in gmprogressive, gmapproach, and gm_1ADD.
Matching-sensitive rewrites were preserved in the new files, including PAD_STACK uses, direct/static helper scoping, and expression choices such as gmapproach using gobj->hsd_obj in fn_801AD920.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large game-mode source, update config/GALE01/splits.txt for every section boundary affected by the split: .text, .data, .bss, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 as applicable.
A file split can make decomp.dev show broken matches in the old source and new matches in the new sources; interpret those as relocation effects when the net linked/matched bytes improve.
Prefer narrower headers after a split: remove moved declarations from the old aggregate header and add per-unit headers that expose only external callbacks or APIs.
Move static structs and static variables to the C file that owns their address range; do not leave private state in an unrelated static header after splitting.
After creating new source files, wire all dependent files to the new headers immediately, especially scene tables and neighboring game-mode code.
Mark new split objects as Matching only after the extracted functions match in their new object context; object boundaries can affect previously nonmatching code.
naming_conventions: New game-mode split files used descriptive lowercase names when a purpose was evident: gmprogressive.c/gmprogressive.h and gmapproach.c/gmapproach.h.
Address-derived names were retained where semantics were less clear or where the split range name remained address-based: gm_1ADD.c and gm_1ADD.h.
Scene lifecycle function suffixes were preserved: _OnFrame, _OnEnter, and _OnLeave.
Internal helper names such as fn_801AD920 and gm_801ADB04 remained address-derived and were made static when only used inside the new file.
Header guards matched the new module names, such as MELEE_GMPROGRESSIVE_H and MELEE_GMAPPROACH_H.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used precise split boundaries: gmprogressive starts at 0x801AD088, gmapproach at 0x801AD920, and gm_1ADD at 0x801ADDD8.
Kept PAD_STACK annotations in matching-sensitive functions, including gmprogressive setup/state functions and gmapproach setup.
Preserved local static storage layout for gm_80480D70, gm_80480D98, gm_803DD4D0, gm_804D6868, and gm_804D686C in the files owning their ranges.
Adjusted helper visibility to static where possible, which can help object-local symbol behavior while keeping public headers clean.
In gmprogressive.c, the switch cases in gm_801AD254 use distinct HSD_SisLib_803A6368 message indices in the new matching version rather than the uniform value shown in the removed gm_1A4C excerpt.
In gmapproach.c, fn_801AD920 uses direct gobj->hsd_obj access for jobj/null checks, an expression-shape detail likely relevant to matching.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported +0.08% matched code (+3104 bytes) and +0.21% linked code (+8048 bytes) for GALE01.
Reported 22 new fully matched functions in gmprogressive, gmapproach, and gm_1ADD.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename gm_1ADD symbols or describe the purpose of the gm_1ADD range.
Search for subsequent cleanup of gmprogressive.h, gmapproach.h, or gm_1ADD.h exports.
Check later PRs touching gmmenu.c to understand why this PR also switched it to Matching.
Look for future decomp.dev reports around gm_1A4C to confirm no section-boundary regressions after this split.
